cards.back: Z = X₀ ⊕ X₁ = X̄₀X₁ + X₀X̄₁. Output 1 when inputs DIFFER. The 'difference detector.'
Sum = A ⊕ B (XOR). Carry = A · B (AND). Two gates for 1-bit addition without carry-in.
Z = (X₀·X₁)̄. Output 0 ONLY when both inputs are 1. Output 1 otherwise. Universal gate.
Z = (X₀+X₁)̄. Output 1 ONLY when both inputs are 0. Output 0 otherwise. Universal gate.
Z = (X₀⊕X₁)̄. Output 1 when inputs are SAME. The 'equality detector.' Complement of XOR.
It can implement NOT, AND, and OR by itself. Only NAND and NOR are universal. XOR is not.
Z = A ⊕ B. When A=0: Z=B (pass). When A=1: Z=B̄ (invert). A controls whether B is inverted.
XOR all bits: result = 1 if odd number of 1s, 0 if even. Used for single-bit error detection.
Tie both NAND inputs together: Z = (A·A)̄ = Ā. Single-input NAND = NOT.
3 NAND gates: NOT A, NOT B (2 single-input NANDs), then NAND(Ā,B̄) = A+B by De Morgan's.
